frontierComparison.interpretation: The open ledger count increased when the 12 q-avoiding source families were replaced by 13 next-obstruction-prime buckets and a larger next q-avoiding rank boundary. That is not global convergence by itself. The useful finite progress is that the 12-family source token has no survivors; the next step must compress the emitted 13 buckets or write an exact ranked boundary instead of descending into singleton q children.

assembledPieces.claim: The p479 bucket-boundary packet closes 19 full-family obstruction-prime buckets covering 422 p479-available residue rows.
assembledPieces.boundary: These terminal bucket closures do not close the partial buckets, q109 q-avoiding descendants, p479-unavailable residues, q97, p443-unavailable residues, or the p4217 complement.

assembledPieces.claim: The q-avoiding batch-cover packet consumes 12 boundary families covering 718 source rows and 9,733,599 q-avoiding classes; every class has a recorded next square-obstruction prime.
assembledPieces.boundary: The cover is a finite source-boundary classification. It does not close the emitted next root children or next q-avoiding rank boundary.

assembledPieces.claim: The batch cover emits 13 next-obstruction-prime buckets with primes from 113 through 191 and 19,467,198 total next root children.
assembledPieces.boundary: The 13 buckets are open theorem-facing families, not singleton repair closures.

assembledPieces.claim: The next q-avoiding layer contains 170,308,883,793 exact descendant rank classes across the 13 buckets.
assembledPieces.boundary: This rank boundary must be compressed, structurally decomposed, or recorded as a deterministic blocker before any child descent.

supportedClaim: For the selected p443/q97/p479-available branch, the 12 nonterminal q-avoiding source families left after q109 subbucketing have no survivors: every one of their 9,733,599 q-avoiding classes lands in a recorded next square-obstruction-prime bucket. The remaining proof surface is the emitted 13-bucket next-prime/rank boundary, not a permission slip for singleton q-child descent.

nextFiniteToken.rankDecreaseIfCompleted: Compress the 13 next-obstruction-prime buckets into theorem-facing structural families or emit an exact deterministic rank boundary; do not consume the token through singleton root-child descent.

compressionCandidate.name: p443_q97_p479_q_avoiding_next_prime_bucket_rank_compression
compressionCandidate.coveredFamily: The 13 next-obstruction-prime buckets emitted by the q-avoiding batch cover, covering 19,467,198 root children and 170,308,883,793 next q-avoiding rank classes.
compressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: The previous 12 source families have already been consumed with zero survivors. The only honest compression target is the emitted 13-bucket rank surface; proving a shared bucket theorem or structural rank decrease would replace millions of root children with one theorem-facing boundary.
compressionCandidate.whyNotSingletonDescent: A singleton q-child step would ignore that the q-avoiding source token was consumed as a batch and would recreate the sibling-ladder anti-pattern at the next rank.
compressionCandidate.failureBoundary: If no shared theorem is visible, emit a deterministic 13-bucket rank-boundary packet with every bucket accounted for and an explicit next finite token.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.exactBoundary: The 13 next-obstruction-prime buckets, 19,467,198 root children, and 170,308,883,793 next q-avoiding rank classes remain outside the assembled proof.

oneNextAction.action: Compress the 13 next-prime buckets into theorem-facing families or a structural rank-decrease packet; if no shared theorem is available, emit the exact deterministic 13-bucket rank boundary before any singleton descent.
oneNextAction.coveredFamily: All 13 next-obstruction-prime buckets emitted by the post-q109 q-avoiding batch cover: 19,467,198 root children and 170,308,883,793 next q-avoiding rank classes.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: A deterministic bucket-rank boundary packet that records all 13 buckets and the exact successor token without opening any individual q-child.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: A single q-prime descent would cover only one emitted child family; bucket compression attempts to account for the whole emitted rank surface and directly addresses the frontier-growth tripwire.
oneNextAction.completionRule: A compression theorem, structural rank-decrease packet, or exact deterministic rank-boundary packet accounts for all 13 emitted buckets and states the next finite token.

proofBoundary: This packet is convergence assembly after a finite q-avoiding batch cover. It proves that the 12 source q-avoiding families have zero survivors only by reference to the checked batch-cover packet. It does not prove the 13 next buckets closed, does not close any unavailable complement, and does not decide Problem 848.
nextTheoremMove: Compress the 13 next-prime buckets, prove a structural rank decrease over the emitted next q-avoiding rank boundary, or emit a deterministic 13-bucket boundary packet before any singleton q-child descent.
